title: Aadhaar Card Services Online - UIDAI Portal
description: Download e-Aadhaar, update Aadhaar card details, check Aadhaar status, and verify Aadhaar online through official UIDAI portal.
overview: UIDAI Aadhaar portal provides complete Aadhaar card services including Aadhaar download PDF, mobile number update, address correction, biometric update, Aadhaar verification, and enrollment status check for Indian residents.
howToApply: 1. Visit official UIDAI website. 2. Select Aadhaar update/download service. 3. Enter Aadhaar number or Enrollment ID. 4. Verify via OTP. 5. Submit request and download confirmation.

title: Income Tax e-Filing Portal - File ITR Online
description: File Income Tax Return (ITR) online, check refund status, download Form 16, and manage PAN details at official Income Tax portal.
overview: Income Tax Department e-Filing portal allows taxpayers to file ITR online, check income tax refund status, link Aadhaar with PAN, download AIS/TIS statements, and pay advance tax securely.
howToApply: 1. Visit Income Tax e-Filing website. 2. Login using PAN. 3. Select correct ITR form. 4. Fill income details. 5. Verify using Aadhaar OTP and submit.

title: GST Registration & Return Filing Portal
description: Apply GST registration online, file GSTR returns, check GST status, and download GST certificate from official GST portal.
overview: GST portal provides complete GST services including new GST registration, GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B return filing, GST payment, GSTIN verification, and compliance tracking for businesses in India.
howToApply: 1. Visit official GST website. 2. Click New Registration. 3. Enter business details and PAN. 4. Upload required documents. 5. Verify via OTP and submit application.

title: Driving License & Vehicle Services - Parivahan Portal
description: Apply for Driving License (DL), Learner License (LL), vehicle registration, RC status check, and vehicle details online at Parivahan portal.
overview: Parivahan Sewa portal by MoRTH offers online services for driving license application, learner license test booking, vehicle registration certificate (RC) status check, vehicle ownership transfer, and challan payment.
howToApply: 1. Visit Parivahan official portal. 2. Select Driving License or Vehicle Services. 3. Fill online application form. 4. Upload documents and pay fee. 5. Book appointment if required.

title: EPFO Portal - Check PF Balance & Withdrawal Online
description: Check PF balance online, submit EPF withdrawal claim, update KYC, and download UAN passbook through official EPFO member portal.
overview: EPFO member portal allows employees to check provident fund balance, file online PF withdrawal claim, update KYC details, activate UAN, and download EPF passbook easily.
howToApply: 1. Visit EPFO member portal. 2. Login using UAN and password. 3. Select Online Services. 4. Submit claim or check balance. 5. Verify via OTP.

title: Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY Health Card Registration
description: Check eligibility for Ayushman Bharat Yojana, download PM-JAY health card, and get ₹5 lakh free treatment benefits online.
overview: PM-JAY (Ayushman Bharat) provides free health insurance coverage up to ₹5 lakh per family per year. Beneficiaries can check eligibility, download Ayushman card, and access cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals.
howToApply: 1. Visit PM-JAY official website. 2. Click Check Eligibility. 3. Enter mobile number or ration card details. 4. Verify OTP. 5. Download or apply for Ayushman card.

title: Update Your Aadhaar Online - UIDAI Address & Demographics Change
description: Update your Aadhaar details online including address change, photo correction, date of birth update, and demographic information through the official UIDAI portal.
overview: The Aadhaar update service allows Indian residents to modify their demographic information such as name, address, date of birth, gender, and also update their photograph and documents online. This service ensures your Aadhaar card remains accurate and up-to-date for seamless verification.
howToApply: 1. Visit myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. 2. Click on 'Update Your Aadhaar'. 3. Login with your Aadhaar number and OTP. 4. Select the details you wish to update. 5. Upload supporting documents. 6. Submit the update request and note the Update Request Number (URN) for tracking.

title: Aadhaar Document Update Online - Upload Proof of Identity & Address
description: Upload and update supporting documents for your Aadhaar, including proof of identity and proof of address, via the official UIDAI portal.
overview: This service enables residents to upload valid supporting documents for Aadhaar validation or updates. It is essential for correcting errors or updating your record with new, valid proofs. The process ensures your demographic data is backed by current and authentic documents.
howToApply: 1. Go to myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. 2. Navigate to 'Update Aadhaar' and login. 3. Choose 'Document Update' service. 4. Select the document type (POI/POA). 5. Upload a clear scan of your document. 6. Submit the request and keep the URN for reference.
documentsRequired: Scanned Copy of Proof of Identity
Scanned Copy of Proof of Address

title: Check Aadhaar Update Status Online - Track URN Status
description: Track the current status of your Aadhaar update request online using your Update Request Number (URN) through the UIDAI portal.
overview: After submitting an Aadhaar update request, residents receive a URN. This service allows you to track whether your update is under process, approved, or rejected. It provides real-time transparency on the progress of your demographic or biometric update.
howToApply: 1. Visit myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. 2. Click on 'Check Aadhaar Update Status'. 3. Enter your 14-digit URN. 4. Enter the security code. 5. Click on 'Check Status' to view the current progress of your request.

title: Update Demographics & Check Status - Aadhaar Data Correction
description: Update your demographic data online and simultaneously check the status of your Aadhaar update request through the UIDAI self-service portal.
overview: This combined service simplifies the process of correcting demographic details like name, address, and gender. It provides a seamless workflow to submit changes and immediately track the application status, ensuring your Aadhaar information is accurate for all official purposes.
howToApply: 1. Login at myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. 2. Select 'Update Demographics Data'. 3. Make necessary changes and upload documents. 4. Submit to generate a URN. 5. Use the same portal to check the status of your submitted request.

title: Download Aadhaar Enrolment & Update Forms - PDF Forms
description: Download official Aadhaar enrolment and update forms in PDF format for offline correction and submission at Aadhaar centres.
overview: For residents who prefer offline methods, UIDAI provides downloadable forms for both new enrolment and updating existing Aadhaar details. These forms can be filled out manually and submitted at any Permanent Enrolment Centre (PEC) for processing.
howToApply: 1. Visit uidai.gov.in/en. 2. Go to the 'Downloads' or 'Forms' section. 3. Select either 'Aadhaar Enrolment Form' or 'Aadhaar Update/Correction Form'. 4. Download the PDF. 5. Print, fill, and submit the form at the nearest Aadhaar centre.

title: Aadhaar Enrolment & Update Charges - Official Fee Structure
description: View the official fee structure for Aadhaar enrolment and updates, including charges for demographic and biometric updates at Aadhaar centres.
overview: UIDAI has a standardized fee for Aadhaar services to ensure transparency. While online updates are often free, physical updates at centres incur a nominal charge. This page details the official cost for enrolment, update, and other related services.
howToApply: 1. Visit uidai.gov.in/en. 2. Navigate to 'Aadhaar Services' or 'Fee Structure'. 3. Check the latest charges for enrolment and updates. 4. Pay the applicable fee at the Aadhaar centre when submitting your request offline.
documentsRequired: No documents required for viewing charges
Payment amount as per fee structure for service

title: Aadhaar Update History Online - View Past Update Requests
description: View the complete history of Aadhaar updates done by a resident, including past URNs and update details, through the official portal.
overview: This service provides a comprehensive log of all updates performed on an Aadhaar number. It helps residents keep track of when and what changes were made to their Aadhaar data, ensuring transparency and helping in cases where historical data is needed.
howToApply: 1. Login at myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in using Aadhaar and OTP. 2. Navigate to 'My Aadhaar' section. 3. Select 'Update History'. 4. View the list of all previous update requests with dates and statuses.

title: Book Aadhaar Appointment Online - Schedule Enrolment Centre Visit
description: Schedule an appointment for Aadhaar enrolment or updates at your nearest Aadhaar centre to avoid waiting in queues.
overview: To streamline the process at Aadhaar Seva Kendras (ASKs), UIDAI offers an online appointment system. Residents can book a slot for enrolment or update services, choose a convenient centre, and visit at the scheduled time for quicker service.
howToApply: 1. Visit bookappointment.uidai.gov.in. 2. Select your state, district, and centre. 3. Choose a service (Enrolment/Update). 4. Pick an available date and time slot. 5. Fill in your details to confirm the appointment and receive an SMS confirmation.
documentsRequired: Supporting Documents for service (e.g., Proof of Identity/Address)

title: Check Aadhaar Enrolment Status - Track New Aadhaar Application
description: Check the status of your new Aadhaar enrolment application online using your 28-digit Enrolment ID (EID).
overview: After enrolling for Aadhaar, it takes a few weeks to process. This status check service allows new applicants to see if their Aadhaar has been generated, is in process, or has been dispatched, using the EID provided on the enrolment slip.
howToApply: 1. Go to myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. 2. Click on 'Check Aadhaar Status'. 3. Enter your 28-digit Enrolment ID and the security code. 4. Click 'Check Status' to see if your Aadhaar is ready for download.

title: Download e-Aadhaar Online - Get Aadhaar PDF
description: Download your e-Aadhaar (PDF format) online from the official UIDAI portal using your Aadhaar number or Enrolment ID.
overview: e-Aadhaar is a digitally signed, password-protected electronic version of your Aadhaar card. It is as valid as the physical PVC card and can be downloaded anytime for online verification, printing, or safekeeping on your mobile device.
howToApply: 1. Visit myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. 2. Select 'Download Aadhaar'. 3. Choose option: By Aadhaar Number or Enrolment ID. 4. Enter details and the security code. 5. Verify via OTP sent to registered mobile. 6. Download the PDF and open it with the password (first 4 letters of name + year of birth).

title: Order Aadhaar PVC Card Online - UIDAI Smart Card
description: Order your official Aadhaar PVC card online for home delivery through the UIDAI portal at a nominal fee.
overview: For residents wanting a durable, high-quality Aadhaar card, UIDAI offers a PVC card service. This card is printed on PVC material and includes a hologram, micro-text, and ghost image for enhanced security and durability, delivered via post.
howToApply: 1. Login at myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. 2. Select 'Order Aadhaar PVC Card'. 3. Verify your details. 4. Make the payment of ₹50 (including GST and postage) online. 5. The card will be dispatched to your address as per Aadhaar records.
documentsRequired: Payment Method (Debit/Credit Card, UPI, Net Banking)

title: Track Aadhaar PVC Card Status - Check Dispatch & Delivery
description: Track the dispatch and delivery status of your ordered Aadhaar PVC card online using your Service Request Number (SRN).
overview: Once you order an Aadhaar PVC card, you receive an SRN. This tracking service allows you to monitor the journey of your card, from printing at the UIDAI facility to dispatch via India Post, and final delivery to your doorstep.
howToApply: 1. Go to myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. 2. Click on 'Check Aadhaar PVC Card Status'. 3. Enter your 14-digit SRN. 4. Enter the security code. 5. View the current status and track the postal shipment.

title: Locate Aadhaar Enrolment Centre Near Me - UIDAI GIS Map
description: Find your nearest Aadhaar enrolment and update centre using the official GIS-based locator tool on the UIDAI website.
overview: UIDAI provides a comprehensive map-based locator to help residents find Permanent Enrolment Centres (PECs) and Aadhaar Seva Kendras (ASKs) in their vicinity. It provides addresses, contact details, and operational hours for easy access.
howToApply: 1. Visit bhuvan.nrsc.gov.in (UIDAI centre locator). 2. Allow location access or enter your state and district. 3. View the map with marked centres. 4. Click on a centre marker for details like address, phone number, and services offered.

title: Verify Aadhaar Number Online - Check Aadhaar Validity
description: Verify if an Aadhaar number is valid and exists in the UIDAI database using the official Aadhaar verification service.
overview: This service allows any individual or organization to verify the authenticity of an Aadhaar number. It confirms whether the number is active, not deactivated, and exists in the CIDR database without revealing any personal details of the holder.
howToApply: 1. Visit myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. 2. Click on 'Verify Aadhaar Number'. 3. Enter the 12-digit Aadhaar number. 4. Enter the security captcha. 5. Click 'Verify' to see if the number is valid.

title: Verify Email or Mobile Number with Aadhaar
description: Verify if your email ID or mobile number is registered and active with your Aadhaar using the UIDAI verification tool.
overview: Having a registered mobile number is crucial for Aadhaar services. This verification tool allows residents to check if their email or mobile is successfully linked to their Aadhaar, ensuring they can receive OTPs and updates without issues.
howToApply: 1. Go to myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. 2. Select 'Verify Email/Mobile Number'. 3. Enter your Aadhaar number and the email/mobile to verify. 4. Enter the captcha. 5. Click 'Verify' to check the status of linkage.
documentsRequired: Email ID or Mobile Number to be verified

title: Retrieve Lost Aadhaar Number or EID Online
description: Recover your forgotten or lost Aadhaar number (UID) or Enrolment ID (EID) online using your name and registered mobile/email.
overview: If you have misplaced your Aadhaar card or enrolment slip, this service helps you retrieve your 12-digit UID or 28-digit EID. It uses your personal details and OTP verification to securely recover the information.
howToApply: 1. Visit myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. 2. Click on 'Retrieve Lost/Forgotten EID/UID'. 3. Choose to retrieve via name & mobile/email. 4. Enter the required details. 5. Verify via OTP. 6. Your UID or EID will be sent to your mobile/email.
documentsRequired: Registered Mobile Number or Email ID

title: Generate Virtual ID (VID) for Aadhaar - Secure Authentication
description: Generate a temporary, 16-digit Virtual ID (VID) to use in place of your Aadhaar number for authentication and verification.
overview: The VID is a security feature that allows you to authenticate your identity without revealing your actual Aadhaar number. It is a temporary, revocable 16-digit number mapped to your Aadhaar, enhancing privacy and security during transactions.
howToApply: 1. Login at myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. 2. Go to 'My Aadhaar' and select 'Virtual ID (VID) Generator'. 3. Click on 'Generate VID'. 4. Verify via OTP. 5. Your new VID will be displayed. You can also retrieve or revoke an existing VID.

title: Get Paperless Offline e-KYC - Aadhaar XML
description: Download your Paperless Offline e-KYC as an encrypted XML file for secure, password-protected sharing with service providers.
overview: This service allows residents to share their identity and address verification with agencies without using biometrics or disclosing the Aadhaar number unnecessarily. The XML file is password-protected and can be uploaded by authorized entities for verification.
howToApply: 1. Login at myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. 2. Select 'Paperless Offline e-KYC'. 3. Enter the last 4 digits of your Aadhaar and OTP. 4. Select the details you wish to share. 5. Download the password-protected ZIP/XML file. (Password is your PIN code).

title: Check Bank Seeding Status with Aadhaar
description: Verify if your Aadhaar number is successfully linked (seeded) with your bank account through the UIDAI portal.
overview: For government subsidies and direct benefit transfers (DBT), Aadhaar needs to be linked to bank accounts. This service allows you to check the seeding status of your Aadhaar with your bank in the NPCI mapper, which is essential for receiving subsidies.
howToApply: 1. Visit myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. 2. Select 'Check Bank Seeding Status'. 3. Enter your Aadhaar number and captcha. 4. Verify via OTP. 5. View the list of banks where your Aadhaar is seeded.

title: Lock/Unlock Aadhaar Biometrics Online
description: Temporarily lock or unlock your Aadhaar biometric authentication to prevent unauthorized use of your fingerprints and iris scans.
overview: This security feature gives residents control over their biometric data. By locking biometrics, you ensure that no authentication requests requiring your fingerprint or iris scan can be processed until you temporarily unlock them, preventing potential misuse.
howToApply: 1. Login at myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. 2. Go to 'My Aadhaar' and select 'Lock/Unlock Biometrics'. 3. Choose to 'Lock' or 'Unlock' your biometrics. 4. Verify the request via OTP. 5. Your biometrics will be locked/unlocked instantly.

title: Login to Income Tax e-Filing Account
description: Secure login to the official Income Tax e-Filing portal. Access your dashboard to file returns, view forms, and manage tax-related services online.
overview: The Income Tax e-Filing portal login is the gateway for taxpayers to access their personalized dashboard. After logging in with your PAN and password, you can file income tax returns (ITR), check the status of filed returns, respond to notices, e-verify returns, view Form 26AS, and manage your profile and pending actions.
howToApply: 1. Visit the official Income Tax e-Filing portal. 2. Enter your PAN/User ID and Password. 3. Enter the Captcha code for verification. 4. Click on 'Login' to access your account dashboard.

title: Register for Income Tax e-Filing (New User)
description: New user registration on the Income Tax e-Filing portal. Create your account to access online tax filing services, link Aadhaar, and more.
overview: Registration on the e-Filing portal is the first step for any taxpayer to access online services. New users need to provide their PAN, which will be validated. Following PAN validation, you provide basic details, set up a password, and register your mobile number. Post-registration, you can log in and start using the portal's services.
howToApply: 1. Go to the e-Filing portal and click on 'Register'. 2. Select user type (Individual, HUF, etc.) and enter PAN. 3. Validate PAN and fill in the registration form with personal details. 4. Set up login credentials and register your mobile number. 5. Complete the registration process and log in.

title: e-Pay Tax - Pay Income Tax Online
description: Pay your income tax online securely through the e-Pay Tax service. Make advance tax, self-assessment tax, or TDS payments via net banking or debit card.
overview: The e-Pay Tax facility on the Income Tax portal allows taxpayers to make various tax payments online. You can pay advance tax, self-assessment tax, regular tax, TDS/TCS (by deductors), and other direct taxes without visiting a bank. The payment is made through internet banking of empanelled banks or debit cards, and a challan is generated instantly.
howToApply: 1. Log in or visit the e-Pay Tax page on the portal. 2. Select the relevant challan (e.g., ITNS 280 for individuals/HUFs). 3. Enter your PAN and other required details. 4. Choose your payment mode (net banking/debit card). 5. Complete the payment and download the challan for your records.
documentsRequired: Details of Tax to be Paid (Assessment Year, Type of Tax)

title: e-Verify Your Income Tax Return
description: e-Verify your filed Income Tax Return (ITR) online using Aadhaar OTP, net banking, bank ATM, or a pre-validated bank/demat account.
overview: e-Verification is a mandatory step after filing an income tax return to complete the filing process. It authenticates that the return was filed by you. The portal offers multiple methods for e-verification including Aadhaar OTP, net banking, DEMAT account, bank account, or generating an EVC. Timely verification ensures your return is processed by the Income Tax Department.
howToApply: 1. Log in to the e-Filing portal. 2. Go to the 'e-File' menu and select 'Income Tax Returns' then 'e-Verify Return'. 3. Choose the return you want to verify. 4. Select your preferred verification method (e.g., Aadhaar OTP). 5. Follow the on-screen instructions to complete verification.
documentsRequired: Access to chosen verification method (e.g., Mobile for Aadhaar OTP, Net Banking)

title: Instant e-PAN - Apply for PAN with Aadhaar
description: Apply for an instant electronic PAN (e-PAN) instantly and free of cost using your Aadhaar card. Get your PAN allotment letter online immediately.
overview: The Instant e-PAN service allows eligible individuals, who possess a valid Aadhaar and have their mobile number linked to it, to apply for a new PAN online. The process is paperless, free of cost, and the PAN is allotted instantly. The e-PAN is a digitally signed PAN card in PDF format, which is a valid proof of PAN.
howToApply: 1. Visit the Instant e-PAN service page. 2. Enter your Aadhaar number and complete OTP validation sent to your Aadhaar-linked mobile. 3. Confirm your basic details (name, DOB, etc.) fetched from Aadhaar. 4. Submit the application. 5. Download your e-PAN allotment letter immediately.

title: Check or Download e-PAN Status
description: Track the status of your Instant e-PAN application or download your e-PAN allotment letter if already processed.
overview: This service allows you to check the processing status of your Instant e-PAN application submitted via the Income Tax portal. If your e-PAN has been allotted, you can download the e-PAN allotment letter directly from this page using your Aadhaar number or application details.
howToApply: 1. Go to the 'Check / Download e-PAN Status' page. 2. Enter your Aadhaar number or the acknowledgement number from your application. 3. Complete the OTP verification sent to your Aadhaar-linked mobile number. 4. The system will display the current status. 5. If allotted, click on the option to download the e-PAN letter.
documentsRequired: Aadhaar Number or Application Acknowledgement Number

title: Verify Your PAN Card Status Online
description: Verify the validity and details of any Permanent Account Number (PAN) online through the Income Tax portal.
overview: The 'Verify PAN' service allows you to check if a PAN is active and valid. By entering the PAN and other basic details like name and date of birth, you can verify the demographic information on record with the Income Tax Department. This is useful for employers, businesses, or individuals to confirm PAN details.
howToApply: 1. Navigate to the 'Verify PAN Status' page. 2. Enter the PAN you wish to verify. 3. Provide additional details like the full name and date of birth as per PAN records. 4. Enter the Captcha code. 5. Click on 'Submit' to view the verification result.

title: Link Aadhaar with PAN Online
description: Link your Aadhaar card with your PAN card online to comply with income tax regulations and avoid your PAN becoming inoperative.
overview: Linking Aadhaar with PAN is mandatory as per income tax laws. This online service on the e-Filing portal allows taxpayers to link their 12-digit Aadhaar number with their PAN. The process is quick, requiring only Aadhaar and PAN details along with OTP validation. Successful linking ensures your PAN remains active and tax returns can be processed.
howToApply: 1. Visit the 'Link Aadhaar with PAN' page. 2. Enter your PAN and Aadhaar number. 3. Confirm your name as per Aadhaar. 4. Validate using the OTP sent to your Aadhaar-linked mobile number. 5. Submit the request. A success message confirms the linking.

title: Check Aadhaar-PAN Linking Status
description: Check whether your Aadhaar card is successfully linked with your PAN card on the Income Tax Department's database.
overview: After submitting a request to link Aadhaar with PAN, you can use this online service to verify if the linking was successful. By entering your PAN and Aadhaar number, the portal will instantly display the current linking status, confirming if the two are linked or not. This helps you ensure compliance.
howToApply: 1. Go to the 'Check Aadhaar-PAN Link Status' page. 2. Enter your PAN and Aadhaar number. 3. Enter the Captcha code for verification. 4. Click on 'View Link Status'. 5. The screen will show a message indicating whether your Aadhaar and PAN are linked.

title: check pan card online Know Your Assessing Officer (AO)
description: Find your jurisdictional Assessing Officer (AO) details including the ward/circle based on your PAN or address.
overview: The 'Know Your AO' service helps taxpayers identify the correct Assessing Officer and the jurisdictional ward/circle where their PAN is registered. This information is crucial for correspondence, submitting specific applications, or understanding which office has jurisdiction over your tax matters.
howToApply: 1. Visit the 'Know Your Assessing Officer (AO)' page. 2. Enter your PAN. 3. Alternatively, search by address details like state, city, and area. 4. Click on 'Submit'. 5. The system will display your AO's details, including the ward/circle and jurisdiction.
documentsRequired: Permanent Account Number (PAN) or Residential Address Details

title: Know Your Tax Payment Status
description: Check the status of your tax challans and verify if your tax payment has been credited to the Income Tax Department.
overview: This service allows taxpayers to verify the status of taxes paid by them (e.g., advance tax, self-assessment tax). By entering the BSR code, challan serial number, and payment amount from your tax payment challan, you can confirm if the payment has been successfully received and credited by the department.
howToApply: 1. Go to the 'Know Tax Payment Status' page. 2. Enter details from your tax challan: BSR Code of the bank branch, Challan Serial Number, and the amount paid. 3. Select the relevant assessment year and challan type (if applicable). 4. Enter the Captcha code. 5. Click 'Submit' to view the status.
documentsRequired: Tax Payment Challan (with BSR Code, Challan No., Amount)

title: Verify Service Request on Income Tax Portal
description: Track and verify the status of service requests submitted on the e-Filing portal, such as PAN update requests or profile changes.
overview: The 'Verify Service Request' feature allows taxpayers to monitor the progress of various service requests they have submitted through the portal. This includes requests for PAN updates, correction in name/date of birth, changes in contact details, or other profile-related services. You can check if the request is pending, processed, or rejected.
howToApply: 1. Visit the 'Verify Service Request' page. 2. Enter your PAN. 3. Provide the Service Request Number (SRN) that was generated when you submitted your request. 4. Enter the Captcha code. 5. Click 'Submit' to view the current status of your request.

title: Report Account Misuse on Income Tax Portal
description: Report any suspected misuse of your account on the Income Tax e-Filing portal or unauthorized access to tax-related information.
overview: This reporting mechanism is for taxpayers who suspect any fraudulent activity, unauthorized access, or misuse of their account or PAN details on the e-Filing portal. By submitting this form, you can alert the Income Tax Department, who can then take necessary action to secure your account and investigate the issue.
howToApply: 1. Navigate to the 'Report Account Misuse' page. 2. Fill in the required details, including your PAN, name, contact information, and a detailed description of the suspected misuse. 3. Provide any supporting information or evidence if available. 4. Submit the report. An acknowledgement will be generated.

title: Authenticate Notice or Order Issued by ITD
description: Verify the authenticity of any notice or order received from the Income Tax Department (ITD) by validating it online.
overview: The 'Authenticate Notice/Order' service helps taxpayers confirm if a notice or order they have received (physically or digitally) is genuinely issued by the Income Tax Department. By entering details from the document, you can cross-verify its existence and validity in the official records, protecting against potential fraud.
howToApply: 1. Go to the 'Authenticate Notice/Order' page. 2. You will need details from the notice or order, such as the Document Identification Number (DIN), PAN, and assessment year. 3. Enter these details along with the Captcha code. 4. Click on 'Submit' to authenticate the document. The system will confirm if it is a valid notice issued by the ITD.
documentsRequired: Document Identification Number (DIN) from the Notice/Order
Assessment Year mentioned in the notice

title: GST Login - Access Your GST Account
description: Secure login to the official GST portal. Access your dashboard to file returns, view ledgers, manage registrations, and track notices.
overview: The GST portal login is the entry point for all registered taxpayers. After logging in with your username and password, you can access the dashboard to file GSTR returns, view electronic cash and liability ledgers, create challans for tax payment, track application status, respond to notices, and manage your GST registration details including amendments and cancellations.
howToApply: 1. Visit the official GST login page. 2. Enter your Username and Password. 3. Enter the Captcha code for verification. 4. Click on 'Login' to access your dashboard.

title: New GST Registration - First Time Login
description: First time login for new GST registrants using your Provisional ID and password. Complete your registration and set up your GST account.
overview: After receiving your Provisional ID from the GST department, new taxpayers must log in for the first time to complete their registration. This process involves validating your Provisional ID, setting up a new password, and providing additional business details. Once completed, your final GSTIN is generated and you can access all taxpayer services.
howToApply: 1. Go to the GST login page and click 'First time login'. 2. Enter your Provisional ID and password received via email. 3. Validate with OTP sent to your registered mobile. 4. Set up a new password and complete the registration form with business details. 5. Submit to generate your permanent GSTIN.

title: Search Taxpayer by GSTIN or PAN
description: Search for any GST-registered taxpayer using their GSTIN or PAN. View public details like legal name, registration status, and business constitution.
overview: The Search Taxpayer service is a public facility on the GST portal that allows anyone to verify the GST registration status of a business or individual. By entering a valid GSTIN (15 characters) or PAN, you can view details such as the legal name of the business, effective date of registration, GSTIN status (active/cancelled), taxpayer type, and the principal place of business. This is useful for vendors, customers, and businesses for verification before transactions.
howToApply: 1. Navigate to the 'Search Taxpayer' page. 2. Select search by GSTIN/UIN or by PAN. 3. Enter the 15-digit GSTIN or 10-digit PAN. 4. Enter the Captcha code. 5. Click 'Search' to view taxpayer details.
documentsRequired: GSTIN or PAN of the taxpayer to be searched

title: Search Taxpayer by UIN (Unique Identification Number)
description: Search for foreign diplomatic missions or UN bodies registered under GST using their Unique Identification Number (UIN).
overview: The Unique Identification Number (UIN) is assigned by the GST department to specialized entities such as foreign diplomatic missions, United Nations organizations, and other notified international organizations. This search service allows users to verify the registration details of such entities by entering their 15-digit UIN, which starts with '99' followed by the 13-digit PAN/other details.
howToApply: 1. Visit the 'Search Taxpayer' page. 2. Select search by GSTIN/UIN. 3. Enter the 15-digit UIN starting with '99'. 4. Enter the Captcha code. 5. Click 'Search' to view the entity's details.
documentsRequired: UIN of the entity to be searched

title: Search HSN Code for GST
description: Search for Harmonized System of Nomenclature (HSN) codes for goods and services to determine correct GST tax rates and classification.
overview: The HSN (Harmonized System of Nomenclature) code search tool helps taxpayers and businesses find the correct code for their goods or services. Accurate HSN codes are essential for filing GST returns, generating e-way bills, and applying the correct tax rates. The search provides HSN codes along with descriptions and can be filtered by chapter, heading, or description.
howToApply: 1. Go to the 'Search HSN Code' page. 2. Enter a keyword, description, or HSN code. 3. Optionally, select a specific chapter to narrow results. 4. Enter the Captcha code. 5. Click 'Search' to view matching HSN codes and descriptions.
documentsRequired: Description of goods/services or partial HSN code

title: Search Taxpayer Return Filing Status
description: Check the return filing status of any GST taxpayer for a specific tax period, including GSTR-3B, GSTR-1, and GSTR-9 filings.
overview: This public service allows users to verify whether a specific taxpayer has filed their GST returns for a given tax period. It provides information on the filing status of major returns like GSTR-3B, GSTR-1, GSTR-5, GSTR-6, and annual returns. This is particularly useful for businesses to check compliance of their suppliers or vendors before claiming input tax credit.
howToApply: 1. Navigate to 'Search Taxpayer Filing Status' page. 2. Enter the taxpayer's GSTIN. 3. Select the financial year and month/quarter for which you want to check filing status. 4. Enter the Captcha code. 5. Click 'Search' to view the status of various returns filed.

title: GST News, Advisories, and Updates
description: View official GST news, press releases, advisories, and updates issued by the GST Council and the Government of India.
overview: The News and Updates section on the GST portal contains all official communications from the GST Council, Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC), and state governments. It includes press releases about council meetings, system advisories, important deadlines, procedural changes, and public notices. Taxpayers should regularly check this section to stay compliant with the latest rules and updates.
howToApply: 1. Visit the GST News and Updates page. 2. Browse through the list of recent news items and advisories. 3. Click on any title to read the full announcement or download attached PDFs. 4. Use filters or search to find specific notifications by date or keyword.
documentsRequired: No documents required - this is a public information page

title: GST Refund Tracking
description: Track the status of your GST refund application online using the Application Reference Number (ARN) or filing period.
overview: The Refund Tracking service allows taxpayers to monitor the progress of their refund claims submitted to the GST department. You can track by entering the ARN (Application Reference Number) generated at the time of filing, or search by filing period and GSTIN. The portal shows the current stage of processing, any deficiencies noted, and the final settlement status of the refund.
howToApply: 1. Go to the GST Refund Tracking page. 2. Choose tracking method: by ARN or by filing period. 3. If by ARN, enter your Application Reference Number. 4. If by period, select financial year, month/quarter, and enter GSTIN. 5. Enter Captcha and click 'Search' to view refund status.
documentsRequired: or GSTIN and filing period details

title: GST Grievance Portal - Lodge Complaint
description: Lodge grievances, register complaints, or seek assistance regarding GST portal issues, taxpayer services, or technical problems.
overview: The GST Self-Service Portal (Grievance) is a dedicated platform for taxpayers and citizens to register complaints and seek resolution for issues related to the GST system. Common grievances include technical glitches on the portal, problems with return filing, registration issues, payment failures, or difficulties in claiming refunds. Each complaint is assigned a unique ticket number for tracking.
howToApply: 1. Visit the GST Self-Service Portal. 2. Click on 'Register Grievance' or 'New Complaint'. 3. Select the category and sub-category of your issue. 4. Provide your GSTIN (if applicable) and contact details. 5. Describe the issue in detail and attach any supporting documents. 6. Submit to receive a complaint reference number for tracking.

title: GST Tutorials and Help Knowledge Base
description: Access official GST tutorials, user manuals, FAQs, and video guides to understand GST processes, return filing, and portal usage.
overview: The GST Tutorial portal is a comprehensive knowledge resource provided by the GSTN to help taxpayers understand various aspects of GST. It contains step-by-step user manuals for different forms and processes, frequently asked questions (FAQs) on various topics, video tutorials explaining return filing, registration, and other key functions, and offline tools guides. This is an essential resource for new and existing taxpayers to learn and resolve common queries.
howToApply: 1. Visit the GST Tutorial website. 2. Browse through categories like Registration, Returns, Payments, Refunds, etc. 3. Click on any topic to view detailed user manuals, FAQs, or video tutorials. 4. Download manuals or watch videos for offline reference.
documentsRequired: No documents required - this is a public knowledge base

title: GST Statistics and Revenue Reports
description: Download GST revenue statistics, monthly collection reports, state-wise collections, and other analytical reports published by the government.
overview: The GST Statistics section provides official data on Goods and Services Tax collections over various periods. It includes monthly collection reports with comparative analysis, state-wise collection breakdowns, year-on-year growth trends, and sectoral contribution data. These reports are published by the Ministry of Finance and are valuable for economic analysis, research, and understanding tax trends.
howToApply: 1. Navigate to the GST Statistics download page. 2. Browse available reports categorized by financial year. 3. Click on the report you wish to download (usually in PDF format). 4. View online or download for offline reference. 5. Some reports may be available in Excel format for analysis.
documentsRequired: No documents required - this is a public data repository

title: GST Taxpayer Dashboard Direct Login
description: Direct authentication page for GST taxpayer dashboard login. Access your GST account securely to manage returns, payments, and compliance.
overview: This direct authentication page provides secure access to the GST taxpayer dashboard. It is functionally similar to the main login page but is specifically the authentication gateway. After successful login, taxpayers can access all services including return filing, viewing ledgers, managing registrations, tracking applications, and responding to notices issued by the department.
howToApply: 1. Visit the GST authentication login page. 2. Enter your Username (GSTIN) and Password. 3. Complete the Captcha verification. 4. Click 'Login' to access your dashboard. 5. For first-time or temporary users, follow the specific login instructions.

title: GST Offline Tools - Returns Utility
description: Download GST offline tools for preparing and filing returns including GSTR-1, GSTR-2, and other utilities without continuous internet connectivity.
overview: The GST Offline Tools allow taxpayers to prepare their return data in an offline environment and upload it later when connected to the internet. The Returns Offline Tool currently supports GSTR-1 and GSTR-2 preparation. It includes features like data entry in CSV templates, validation checks, and generation of JSON files for upload. The tool also comes with user manuals and sample templates to guide taxpayers through the process.
howToApply: 1. Visit the GST Offline Tools download page. 2. Download the Returns Offline Tool (zip file). 3. Extract the zip file to a folder on your computer. 4. Read the 'Readme' document and user manual before installation. 5. Run the installer and follow on-screen instructions. 6. Use the tool to prepare return data offline and generate upload files.
documentsRequired: GSTIN and business data for return preparation

title: GST Laws, Acts, and Rules
description: Access the complete GST Act, rules, regulations, and legal framework governing Goods and Services Tax in India.
overview: The GST Law section contains the complete legal framework for Goods and Services Tax in India. It includes the Central GST (CGST) Act, Integrated GST (IGST) Act, Union Territory GST (UTGST) Act, and GST (Compensation to States) Act. Additionally, it provides access to GST rules, regulations, notifications related to legal provisions, and amendments to the acts. This is an essential resource for legal professionals, tax practitioners, and businesses needing to understand compliance requirements.
howToApply: 1. Visit the GST Law page. 2. Browse through the categories: Acts, Rules, Regulations, or Notifications. 3. Click on the document title to view or download the PDF. 4. Use the search function to find specific sections or amendments. 5. Check for updates and new notifications regularly.
documentsRequired: No documents required - this is a public legal information page

title: GST e-Way Bill System
description: Generate, manage, and track e-way bills for movement of goods under GST. Login to the National e-Way Bill Portal for all e-way bill services.
overview: The e-Way Bill System is a separate portal integrated with GST for the generation of electronic waybills required for the movement of goods valued above ₹50,000. Registered taxpayers can generate Part-A and Part-B of e-way bills, extend validity, manage consignments, and track vehicle movements. The system also allows for generating consolidated e-way bills, handling rejections, and managing transporter IDs.
howToApply: 1. Visit the National e-Way Bill Portal. 2. Login using your GSTIN and password (separate registration may be required). 3. Select 'Generate New E-Way Bill' and enter invoice details, transporter details, and goods description. 4. Generate Part-A and Part-B of the bill. 5. Share the EBN (E-Way Bill Number) with the transporter and verify on the portal if needed.

title: Udyam Registration - MSME Registration Portal
description: Official Udyam Registration portal for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME). Register your business online for Udyam certificate, free of cost.
overview: Udyam Registration is the government's online registration process for MSMEs. It is free, paperless, and based on self-declaration using Aadhaar. On registration, the enterprise is assigned a permanent Udyam Registration Number and an e-certificate with a dynamic QR code. The portal is fully integrated with Income Tax and GSTIN systems to automatically fetch investment and turnover details.
howToApply: 1. Visit the official Udyam Registration portal. 2. Click on 'For New Entrepreneurs' and select 'Register as Udyam'. 3. Enter your Aadhaar number and verify via OTP. 4. Validate your PAN (required for registration from 01.04.2021). 5. Fill in the enterprise details, NIC codes, and bank information. 6. Submit the self-declaration form to generate your Udyam Registration Number and Certificate.

title: Udyam Login - Access Your Udyam Account
description: Login to your Udyam account using Udyam Registration Number and mobile OTP to update information, download certificate, or manage registration.
overview: The Udyam Login portal allows registered MSMEs to access their account securely. After login, you can update your enterprise details, view/download your Udyam Registration Certificate, update investment and turnover information for re-classification, and manage your profile. The login requires your 19-digit Udyam Registration Number and OTP verification on your registered mobile number.
howToApply: 1. Go to the Udyam Login page. 2. Enter your 19-digit Udyam Registration Number (format: UDYAM-XX-00-0000000). 3. Enter your registered Mobile Number. 4. Choose OTP option (mobile/email) and click 'Validate & Generate OTP'. 5. Enter the OTP received and click 'Validate OTP & Login' to access your dashboard.

title: Verify Udyam Registration Number
description: Verify the authenticity of any Udyam Registration Certificate by searching with the Udyam Registration Number or entrepreneur details.
overview: This public verification service allows anyone to check the validity and details of an MSME's Udyam Registration. By entering the Udyam Registration Number or entrepreneur name and location, you can view the enterprise's registration status, legal name, business activity, and other public details. This is useful for vendors, customers, and government agencies to verify MSME credentials.
howToApply: 1. Visit the Udyam Verify page. 2. Search by either Udyam Registration Number or Entrepreneur Details. 3. If searching by number, enter the 19-digit Udyam Registration Number. 4. If searching by details, enter entrepreneur name, state, and district. 5. Enter the Captcha code and click 'Search' to view registration details.
documentsRequired: Udyam Registration Number OR Entrepreneur Name with location

title: Print Udyog Aadhaar Memorandum (UAM) Certificate
description: Print your Udyog Aadhaar Memorandum (UAM) Certificate for old registrations by entering UAM number and registered mobile number.
overview: This service is for enterprises registered under the earlier Udyog Aadhaar Memorandum (UAM) system. You can print your UAM certificate by providing the 12-digit UAM number and the mobile number used during registration. Note: Existing UAM registrations were required to re-register under the new Udyam system by December 31, 2021.
howToApply: 1. Go to the Print UAM Certificate page. 2. Enter your 12-digit UAM Number (format: DL05A0000001). 3. Enter the Mobile Number registered with your UAM. 4. Click on 'Print Certificate' to view and download your UAM certificate.

title: Print Udyog Aadhaar Application Data
description: Print the application data submitted during Udyog Aadhaar Memorandum (UAM) registration using UAM number and mobile number.
overview: This service allows you to retrieve and print the complete application data that was submitted during your Udyog Aadhaar Memorandum registration. It displays all information provided at the time of registration including enterprise details, address, activities, and contact information. This is useful for record-keeping or reference.
howToApply: 1. Visit the Print UAM Application page. 2. Enter your 12-digit UAM Number (format: DL05A0000001). 3. Enter the Mobile Number registered with your UAM. 4. Click on 'Print Application' to view and download your complete application data.

title: Verify Udyog Aadhaar (UAM) Registration
description: Verify the authenticity of an old Udyog Aadhaar Memorandum (UAM) registration using UAM number or entrepreneur details.
overview: This public verification service allows you to check the validity of Udyog Aadhaar Memorandum registrations (the system prior to Udyam). You can search by UAM number or by entrepreneur name and location to view registration details. Note: UAM registrations are being phased out and enterprises are encouraged to register under the new Udyam system.
howToApply: 1. Go to the Verify Udyog Aadhaar page. 2. Search by either UAM Number or Entrepreneur Details. 3. If searching by number, enter the 12-digit UAM Number. 4. If searching by details, enter entrepreneur name, state, and district. 5. Enter the Captcha code and click 'Search' to view registration details.
documentsRequired: UAM Number (12-digit) OR Entrepreneur Name with location

title: Forgot Udyam/UAM Number - Retrieve Lost Registration Number
description: Retrieve your forgotten Udyam Registration Number or Udyog Aadhaar Memorandum (UAM) number using Aadhaar or PAN details.
overview: If you have lost or forgotten your Udyam Registration Number or old UAM number, this service helps you retrieve it. You can search using your Aadhaar number (for Udyam) or PAN/other details (for UAM). The system will display your registration number after successful verification, allowing you to access or print your certificate.
howToApply: 1. Visit the Forgot Udyam/UAM Number page. 2. Select whether you want to retrieve Udyam or UAM number. 3. For Udyam, enter your Aadhaar number and verify via OTP. 4. For UAM, enter your PAN or other identifying details. 5. After verification, your registration number will be displayed.
documentsRequired: For Udyam: Aadhaar Number and OTP verification
For UAM: PAN or other registration details

title: UAM to Udyam Conversion - Migrate Old Registration
description: Convert your existing Udyog Aadhaar Memorandum (UAM) registration to the new Udyam registration system online.
overview: Enterprises registered under the earlier UAM system are required to migrate to the new Udyam registration system. This conversion process allows you to transfer your existing registration to the new format. The process is free and uses your Aadhaar and PAN details to fetch updated information. After conversion, you receive a new Udyam Registration Number and Certificate.
howToApply: 1. Go to the UAM to Udyam Conversion page. 2. Enter your existing UAM number. 3. Provide your Aadhaar number and verify via OTP. 4. Validate your PAN (required). 5. Review and confirm auto-filled details from government databases. 6. Submit to generate your new Udyam Registration Number and Certificate.

title: Benefits of Udyam Registration - MSME Schemes
description: Download the official document detailing all benefits available to Udyam-registered MSMEs including subsidies, schemes, and incentives.
overview: This document provides comprehensive information about the various benefits available to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises registered under Udyam. Benefits include priority sector lending, collateral-free loans, interest subsidies, tax exemptions, government tender participation, protection against delayed payments, and various schemes run by the Ministry of MSME. The PDF document serves as an official reference for MSMEs to understand their entitlements.
howToApply: 1. Click on the Benefits of Udyam Registration link. 2. The PDF document will open or download automatically. 3. Save the document for future reference. 4. Review the various schemes and benefits applicable to your enterprise category.
documentsRequired: No documents required - this is a downloadable information PDF

title: Udyam Registration Portal - Ministry Overview
description: Main information portal of the Ministry of MSME with access to Udyam registration, NIC codes, documents, circulars, and verification services.
overview: This is the central information hub for the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises. It provides access to all Udyam-related services including registration, login, verification, printing certificates, and updating details. The portal also hosts important documents like Udyam sample forms, NIC codes, circulars and orders, and bulletins. It serves as the single point of truth for MSME registration and related information.
howToApply: 1. Visit the Ministry overview page. 2. Navigate through the menu to access various services: Udyam Registration, Login, Verify, Print Certificates, etc. 3. Access 'Useful Documents' for downloading forms and guidelines. 4. Check 'Circulars & Orders' for latest notifications. 5. Use the language selector to view the portal in your preferred language.
documentsRequired: No documents required - this is a navigation portal

title: Udyam Registration Sample Application Form
description: Download the sample Udyam Registration application form PDF to understand the fields and information required before starting online registration.
overview: This sample form provides a preview of the Udyam Registration application process. It shows all the fields and information required, including Aadhaar verification section, PAN details, enterprise information, social category, gender, divyang status, plant locations, official address, bank details, and NIC code selection. Reviewing this sample helps entrepreneurs prepare all necessary information before starting the actual online registration.
howToApply: 1. Click on the Udyam Registration Sample Form link. 2. The PDF document will open or download automatically. 3. Review all sections of the form to understand required information. 4. Gather all necessary details and documents as shown in the sample. 5. Proceed to the actual online registration with prepared information.
documentsRequired: No documents required - this is a sample form for reference

title: NIC Code for MSME Classification - Complete List
description: Download the complete National Industrial Classification (NIC) 2008 code list for MSME registration, including all economic activities.
overview: This official document from the Central Statistical Organisation contains the complete National Industrial Classification (NIC) 2008, which is used for classifying economic activities during Udyam Registration. It includes detailed codes at 2-digit, 4-digit, and 5-digit levels for manufacturing, services, and trading activities. The document also provides explanatory notes and concordance tables to help entrepreneurs correctly identify their business activity codes for MSME registration.
howToApply: 1. Click on the NIC Code document link. 2. The comprehensive PDF will open or download. 3. Navigate to the relevant section based on your business type (manufacturing/services/trading). 4. Find the appropriate 5-digit NIC code that best describes your business activity. 5. Note down the code for use during Udyam Registration.
documentsRequired: No documents required - this is a reference document

title: Update or Cancel Udyam Registration
description: Update your Udyam Registration details or cancel registration due to business closure, using your Udyam login account.
overview: Registered MSMEs can update their information or cancel their Udyam registration through this service. Updates may include changes in investment, turnover (required annually for re-classification), contact details, or business activities. In case of business closure, enterprises can apply for cancellation of their Udyam registration. Both services require login with Udyam Registration Number and OTP verification.
howToApply: 1. Go to the Udyam Login page. 2. Login with your 19-digit Udyam Registration Number and OTP. 3. For updates, navigate to 'Update Details' and modify the required fields. 4. For annual turnover/investment updates, follow the prompts for re-classification. 5. For cancellation, select 'Cancel Registration' and provide reason for closure. 6. Submit changes; updated certificate will be generated or cancellation confirmed.
